title: Witch Week - Chapter 15 (Fragment 5)
text:
Nan was nothing like ready. She was about to say so, when the classroom door flew open. Mr. Wentworth was barged aside. And Miss Cadwallader stood in his place, stiff and upright and stringy with anger.

“What are you all doing, 6B?” she said. “Back to your seats with the utmost rapidity.”

Mr. Wentworth was behind the door, white and shaking. Everyone looked doubtfully at Chrestomanci. He had gone very vague. So everybody did the prudent thing and scuttled back to their desks. As they went, three more people came into the room behind Miss Cadwallader.

Miss Cadwallader faced Chrestomanci in angry triumph. “Mr. Chant,” she said, “you are an imposter. Here is the real inquisitor. Inquisitor Littleton.” She stood aside and shut the door, so that everyone could see the inquisitor.

Inquisitor Littleton was a small man in a blue pin-striped suit. He had a huge man on either side of him in the black uniform of the Inquisition. Each of these huge men had a gun holster, a truncheon, and a folded whip in his belt. At the sight of them, Charles’s burned finger doubled itself up and hid inside his fist like a guilty secret.

“You move, and I’ll order you shot!” Inquisitor Littleton snapped at Chrestomanci. His voice was harsh. His little watery eyes glared at Chrestomanci from a little blunt face covered with bright red veins. His blue suit did not fit him very well, as if Inquisitor Littleton had shrunk and hardened some time after the suit was bought, into a new shape, dense with power.

“Good afternoon, inquisitor,” Chrestomanci said politely. “I’d been half expecting you.” He looked across his shoulder, to Simon. “Nod, if I’m right,” he said. “Did you say an inquisitor would be here before lunch?”

Simon nodded, looking shattered.

Inquisitor Littleton narrowed his watery eyes. “So it was witchcraft that made my car break down?” he said. “I knew it!” He unslung a black box he was carrying on a strap over his shoulder. He pointed it at Chrestomanci and turned a knob. Everyone saw the violent twitching of the dials on top. “Thought so,” grated Inquisitor Littleton. “It’s a witch.” He jerked his blunt chin at Mr. Wentworth. “Now get me that one.”

One of the huge men reached out a huge hand and dragged Mr. Wentworth over from beside the door as easily as if Mr. Wentworth had been a guy stuffed with straw. Miss Cadwallader looked as if she would like to protest about this, but she gave it up as useless. Inquisitor Littleton trained his black box on Mr. Wentworth.

Before he could turn the knob, the black box was torn out of his hands. With its broken strap trailing, it hurried from the inquisitor to Chrestomanci.

“I think that was a mistake, Brian,” said Chrestomanci.

Both huge men drew their guns. Inquisitor Littleton backed away and pointed at Chrestomanci. His face was purple, and full of a queer mixture of hate and horror and pleasure. “Look at that!” he shouted out. “It has a demon to wait on it! Oh, I’ve got you now!”

Chrestomanci looked almost irritated. “My good man,” he said, “that really is a most ignorant assumption. Only a hedge wizard would stoop to using a demon.”

“I’m not a demon!” shouted Brian’s shrill voice. “I’m Brian Wentworth!”

Delia screamed. The huge man who was not holding Mr. Wentworth seemed to lose his nerve. Glaring with fear, he held his gun out in both hands, and aimed it at the black box.

“Throw it!” said Chrestomanci.

Brian obeyed. The black box sailed towards the window. The huge man was muddled into following it around with his gun. He fired. There was a tremendous crash. Quite a few people screamed this time. The black box exploded into a muddle of wire and metal plates and half of the window blew out. A gust of rain blew in.

vocabulary.definition: a thing that is accepted as true or as certain to happen, without proof.
vocabulary.example: “My good man,” he said, “that really is a most ignorant assumption.

vocabulary.definition: to happen suddenly or start unexpectedly.

vocabulary.definition: closely compacted in substance.
vocabulary.example: His blue suit did not fit him very well, as if Inquisitor Littleton had shrunk and hardened some time after the suit was bought, into a new shape, dense with power.

vocabulary.definition: to pull someone or something forcibly from one place to another.
vocabulary.example: ” He unslung a black box he was carrying on a strap over his shoulder.

vocabulary.definition: to burst or shatter violently and noisily.
vocabulary.example: The black box exploded into a muddle of wire and metal plates and half of the window blew out.

vocabulary.definition: to look at someone or something in an angry or fierce way.
vocabulary.example: His little watery eyes glared at Chrestomanci from a little blunt face covered with bright red veins.

vocabulary.definition: to make an unpleasant, harsh, or grating sound.
vocabulary.example: “Thought so,” grated Inquisitor Littleton.

vocabulary.definition: a secret one feels shame or remorse about.
vocabulary.example: At the sight of them, Charles’s burned finger doubled itself up and hid inside his fist like a guilty secret.

vocabulary.definition: to make or become firm, stiff, or solid.
vocabulary.example: His blue suit did not fit him very well, as if Inquisitor Littleton had shrunk and hardened some time after the suit was bought, into a new shape, dense with power.

vocabulary.definition: a holder for a pistol or other small firearm, typically made of leather and worn on a belt.
vocabulary.example: Each of these huge men had a gun holster, a truncheon, and a folded whip in his belt.

vocabulary.definition: lacking knowledge or awareness.
vocabulary.example: “My good man,” he said, “that really is a most ignorant assumption.

vocabulary.definition: a person who pretends to be someone else in order to deceive others.
vocabulary.example: Chant,” she said, “you are an imposter.

vocabulary.definition: a period of prolonged and intensive questioning or investigation, historically a judicial institution.
vocabulary.example: He had a huge man on either side of him in the black uniform of the Inquisition.

vocabulary.definition: a person who conducts an official investigation or inquiry, especially one with stern or harsh questioning.
vocabulary.example: Here is the real inquisitor.

vocabulary.definition: confused and disorganized.
vocabulary.example: The huge man was muddled into following it around with his gun.

vocabulary.definition: to become or make more limited in extent or width.
vocabulary.example: Inquisitor Littleton narrowed his watery eyes.

vocabulary.definition: acting with or showing care and thought for the future.
vocabulary.example: So everybody did the prudent thing and scuttled back to their desks.

vocabulary.definition: to move quickly with short steps, especially to retreat or return hurriedly.
vocabulary.example: “Back to your seats with the utmost rapidity.

vocabulary.definition: having a high-pitched and piercing sound or voice.
vocabulary.example: ” shouted Brian’s shrill voice.

vocabulary.definition: to speak abruptly or angrily to someone.
vocabulary.example: Everyone looked doubtfully at Chrestomanci.

vocabulary.definition: not easily bent or changed; rigid.
vocabulary.example: And Miss Cadwallader stood in his place, stiff and upright and stringy with anger.

vocabulary.definition: to lower oneself to do something considered beneath one's dignity.
vocabulary.example: She was about to say so, when the classroom door flew open.

vocabulary.definition: consisting of or resembling strings; thin and tough.
vocabulary.example: And Miss Cadwallader stood in his place, stiff and upright and stringy with anger.

vocabulary.definition: very great in amount, scale, or intensity.
vocabulary.example: There was a tremendous crash.

vocabulary.definition: a great victory or achievement.
vocabulary.example: Miss Cadwallader faced Chrestomanci in angry triumph.

vocabulary.definition: a short, thick stick carried by a police officer as a weapon.
vocabulary.example: Each of these huge men had a gun holster, a truncheon, and a folded whip in his belt.

vocabulary.definition: making small, sudden jerking movements.
vocabulary.example: Everyone saw the violent twitching of the dials on top.

vocabulary.definition: standing or sitting with a straight vertical posture.
vocabulary.example: And Miss Cadwallader stood in his place, stiff and upright and stringy with anger.

vocabulary.definition: a strip of leather or other material fastened to a handle, used for flogging or beating.
vocabulary.example: Each of these huge men had a gun holster, a truncheon, and a folded whip in his belt.
